task_summary: SP21d implements a bounded mew-wisp compact terminal HUD slice under experiments/mew-ghost. Normal --format human output, including --form cat, now emphasizes a compact title/HUD/focus/signal/next surface while diagnostic freshness, desk details/counts, active-window reason, and launcher intents are opt-in behind --details. The slice keeps mew connection work out of scope and preserves state JSON, HTML, watch, live-read gates, launcher safety, default human form behavior, and the cat sprite similarity contract.
proof_artifacts: applied compact HUD source edit tool #1101
applied README edit and focused pytest verification tool #1103

verification_evidence.summary: 35 passed after applying the compact HUD renderer, --details flag, focused output/watch/README tests, and preservation checks.

implemented_contract.compact_human_title: Normal --format human starts with mew-wisp compact terminal HUD instead of the older debug-log-like terminal human view title.
implemented_contract.compact_default_form: The implicit human renderer and --form default remain equivalent and render the compact HUD without cat-only headings.
implemented_contract.compact_lines: The compact human surface presents a concise HUD line plus state marker, focus, signal, and next lines.
implemented_contract.cat_form_preserved: --format human --form cat still prepends the terminal form metadata and 22x24 block-cell cat sprite, keeps the state marker outside the silhouette, and preserves the similarity threshold tests.
implemented_contract.details_flag: --details is the explicit opt-in for expanded human diagnostics.
implemented_contract.diagnostics_hidden_by_default: Freshness/watch iteration text, desk details/counts, active-window reason, and launcher intents are hidden from normal human/cat output unless --details is requested.
implemented_contract.diagnostics_available: When --details is requested, the human cat output includes details, freshness, desk details, active window, and launcher intents sections for debugging.
implemented_contract.watch_behavior_preserved: Bounded and interrupt-driven human watch continue to print terminal surfaces rather than JSONL records, now using the compact HUD surface by default.
implemented_contract.state_json_preserved: --format state JSON behavior remains covered by the focused test module and is not replaced by the HUD rendering.
implemented_contract.html_preserved: HTML output and bounded HTML watch behavior remain covered by the focused test module and are outside the human HUD presentation change.
implemented_contract.live_and_launcher_safety_preserved: The slice does not add mew connection work and keeps live-active-window/live-desk opt-in gates plus launcher execution safety checks covered by existing tests.

covered_cases: desk fixture human output starts with the compact HUD and omits launcher intents and desk diagnostics by default
human bounded watch prints compact HUD surfaces without freshness/debug lines or JSONL record markers
human unbounded watch until KeyboardInterrupt prints compact HUD surfaces without watch-iteration freshness lines
default human form equals implicit human form and remains compact
cat form keeps terminal form metadata, cat state metadata, 22x24 sprite similarity, and state marker outside the sprite
--details on human cat output includes diagnostic details, freshness, desk details, active window, and launcher intents
README documents compact human/cat watch usage and --details diagnostic usage
source isolation test covers --details alongside live-read and launcher safety flags

acceptance_notes: All product, test, doc, and report changes are scoped under experiments/mew-ghost.
No mew connection work is included in this slice.
The local side-dogfood report records the SP21d practical compact terminal HUD evidence.
remaining_risk: Reviewer follow-up SP21e removed a duplicate cat HUD state marker and kept this as a mew-authored repair, not a Codex product-code rescue.
